1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle and set apart for the good news of God.
God promised this good news long ago through his prophets in the holy Scriptures.
It is about his Son. As a human being, the Son was born from the family of David.
And by the Holy Spirit, he was shown to be the Son of God with power when he rose from death — Jesus Christ our Lord.
Through Christ I received grace and the work of an apostle, to call people of all nations to the obedience that comes from faith, for the honor of his name.
And you also are among those who are called to belong to Jesus Christ.
To all of you in Rome whom God loves and has called to be his holy people: May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because people all over the world are talking about your faith.
God knows that I always remember you in my prayers. He is the One I serve with my whole heart by spreading the good news about his Son.
And I always ask that, if it is God's will, I may at last find a way to come to you.
I long to see you, so that I may share with you a spiritual gift to make you strong —
or rather, so that you and I may encourage each other through our shared faith.
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that I planned many times to come to you, but until now I have been kept from it. I wanted to see good results among you, as I have among the other nations.
I have a duty to everyone — to Greeks and to those who are not Greeks, to the wise and to the foolish.
So I am eager to preach the good news to you also who are in Rome.
I am not ashamed of the good news, because it is the power of God that brings salvation to everyone who believes — first to the Jew, and also to the Greek.
For in the good news, God shows how he makes people right with himself, a rightness that is by faith from start to finish. As the Scripture says: The person who is right with God will live by faith.
The anger of God is shown from heaven against all the godlessness and evil of people who hold back the truth by their evil.
They know what can be known about God, because God has made it plain to them.

Ever since God made the world, his invisible qualities — his eternal power and his divine nature — have been clearly seen. People understand them from the things God has made. So people have no excuse.
Even though they knew God, they did not honor him as God or give him thanks. Instead, their thinking became worthless, and their foolish hearts became dark.
They claimed to be wise, but they became fools.
They traded the glory of the God who never dies for images that look like people, who do die, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
So God let them go after the sinful desires of their hearts, which led to shameful things done with their bodies.
They traded the truth of God for a lie, and they worshiped and served created things instead of the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
Because of this, God let them go after shameful desires. Even the women traded natural relations for unnatural ones.

In the same way, the men stopped having natural relations with women and burned with desire for one another. Men did shameful things with men, and they suffered in themselves the punishment they deserved for their wrong.
Since they did not think it worthwhile to keep knowing God, God let them go after their worthless thinking, so that they did what they should not do.
They became full of every kind of evil, sin, greed, and hate. They were full of jealousy, murder, fighting, lying, and meanness. They were gossips,
those who say evil things about others, haters of God, rude, proud, and boastful. They thought up new ways to do evil. They disobeyed their parents.
They were senseless, they broke their promises, they had no love, and they showed no mercy.
They know God's just rule, that those who do such things deserve death. Yet they not only keep doing them, but they also approve of others who do them.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So you have no excuse, whoever you are, when you judge someone else. For when you judge another person, you are condemning yourself, because you do the same things.
We know that God judges fairly those who do such things.
So do you think — you who judge others but do the same things — that you will escape God's judgment?
Or do you look down on the riches of God's kindness, patience, and self-control? Do you not know that God's kindness is meant to lead you to change your heart?
But because you are stubborn and refuse to change, you are storing up anger against yourself for the day of anger, when God's fair judgment will be shown.
God will pay back each person for what he has done.
To those who keep on doing good, seeking glory, honor, and life that never ends, he will give eternal life.
But for those who are selfish, who reject the truth and follow evil, there will be anger and fury.
There will be trouble and suffering for everyone who does evil — first for the Jew, and also for the Greek.
But there will be glory, honor, and peace for everyone who does good — first for the Jew, and also for the Greek.
For God treats everyone the same.
All who sin without knowing the law of Moses will also be lost without the law. And all who sin under the law will be judged by the law.
For it is not those who hear the law who are right with God, but those who obey the law who will be made right.
People who are not Jews do not have the law. But when they do by nature what the law requires, they are a law for themselves, even though they do not have the law.
They show that the demands of the law are written on their hearts. Their conscience agrees, and their own thoughts sometimes accuse them and sometimes defend them.
This will all be clear on the day when God judges people's secrets through Christ Jesus, as my good news says.
Now you call yourself a Jew. You depend on the law and brag that you are close to God.
You know what God wants, and because the law has taught you, you can tell what is best.
You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for those in the dark,
a teacher of the foolish and of the young, because in the law you have the full shape of knowledge and truth.
So you who teach others, do you not teach yourself? You preach that people should not steal — do you steal?
You say people should not commit adultery — do you commit adultery? You hate idols — do you rob temples?
You brag about the law — do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
As the Scripture says: Because of you, God's name is insulted among the other nations.
Being circumcised has value if you obey the law. But if you break the law, you might as well not be circumcised at all.
So if those who are not circumcised obey what the law requires, will they not be counted as if they were circumcised?
The one who is not circumcised in body but obeys the law will judge you who break the law, even though you have the written law and are circumcised.
A real Jew is not just one on the outside, and real circumcision is not only on the outside, in the body.
No, a real Jew is one on the inside, and real circumcision is of the heart, done by the Spirit, not by the written law. Such a person is praised by God, not by people.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So what advantage is there in being a Jew? Or what good is circumcision?
There is great advantage in every way! First of all, the Jews were trusted with the very words of God.
What if some of them were not faithful? Will their lack of faith cancel God's faithfulness?
No, never! Let God be true, even if everyone else is a liar. As the Scripture says: So you will be shown right when you speak, and you will win when you are judged.
But if our wrong brings out how right God is, what should we say? That God is unfair to punish us? (I am speaking the way people do.)
No, never! If that were so, how could God judge the world?
Someone might argue: If my lie makes God's truth shine even more, to his glory, then why am I still judged as a sinner?
Why not just say: Let us do evil so that good may come? Some people slander us by claiming we say this. They deserve to be judged.
So what then? Are we Jews any better? Not at all. We have already shown that everyone, Jews and non-Jews alike, is under the power of sin.
As the Scripture says: There is no one who is right with God, not even one.
There is no one who understands; there is no one who looks for God.
Everyone has turned away; together they have become useless. There is no one who does good, not even one.
Their throats are like open graves; their tongues tell lies. The poison of snakes is on their lips.
Their mouths are full of curses and bitter words.
They are quick to kill.
They leave ruin and misery wherever they go,
and they do not know the way of peace.
They have no fear of God at all.
Now we know that whatever the law says, it says to those who are under the law. So no one can make any excuse, and the whole world is held guilty before God.
No one will be made right with God by obeying the law. The law only makes us aware of our sin.
But now, apart from the law, God has shown how he makes people right with himself. The law and the prophets pointed to this.
God makes people right with himself through faith in Jesus Christ. This is for everyone who believes; there is no difference between people,
because everyone has sinned and falls short of the glory of God.
Yet by his grace, as a free gift, God makes them right with himself, through Christ Jesus, who set them free.
God offered Christ as the sacrifice that takes away sin, through faith in his blood. God did this to show that he is right, because in his patience he had let earlier sins go unpunished.
He did it to show his rightness at the present time, so that he could be both fair and the One who makes right anyone who has faith in Jesus.
So where is there any room for bragging? There is none. On what basis is it shut out? On the basis of obeying the law? No, but on the basis of faith.
For we hold that a person is made right with God by faith, apart from obeying the law.
Or is God the God of the Jews only? Is he not also the God of the other nations? Yes, of them too,
since there is only one God. He will make the circumcised right by faith, and the uncircumcised right through that same faith.
So do we cancel the law by this faith? No, never! Instead, we uphold the law.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So what shall we say about Abraham, the father of our people? What did he discover?
If Abraham was made right with God by his works, then he had something to brag about — but not before God.
For what does the Scripture say? Abraham believed God, and because of that he was accepted as right with God.
Now when a person works, his pay is not counted as a gift, but as something owed to him.
But to the person who does not work, but trusts God, who makes the ungodly right with himself, his faith is counted as making him right with God.
David says the same thing when he speaks of the happiness of the person whom God counts as right, apart from works:
Happy are those whose wrongs are forgiven, whose sins are covered.
Happy is the person whose sin the Lord will never count against him.
Is this happiness only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? We have been saying that Abraham's faith was counted as making him right with God.
So when did this happen? Was it after he was circumcised, or before? It was before, not after!

Abraham received circumcision later as a sign, a seal of the rightness he already had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. So he is the father of all who believe but are not circumcised, so that they too can be counted right with God.
And he is also the father of the circumcised — not only those who are circumcised, but those who also follow in the steps of the faith our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.
God's promise that Abraham would inherit the world did not come through the law. It came through the rightness that is by faith.
For if those who depend on the law are the heirs, then faith means nothing and the promise is worthless.
The law brings God's anger, but where there is no law, there is no breaking of the law.

So the promise comes by faith, so that it may be a gift of grace, guaranteed to all of Abraham's family — not only to those who have the law, but also to those who have the faith of Abraham. He is the father of us all.

As the Scripture says: I have made you the father of many nations. Abraham is our father in God's sight, the God he believed — the God who gives life to the dead and calls into being things that did not exist.
Against all hope, Abraham believed in hope, and so he became the father of many nations, just as he had been told: That is how many your descendants will be.
His faith did not weaken, even though he was about a hundred years old and his body was as good as dead, and Sarah was too old to have children.
He did not doubt God's promise through unbelief. Instead, his faith made him strong, and he gave glory to God,
being fully sure that God had the power to do what he had promised.
That is why his faith was counted as making him right with God.
The words “it was counted to him” were not written for Abraham alone,
but also for us. Our faith will be counted in the same way, since we believe in the God who raised Jesus our Lord from death.
Jesus was handed over to die for our sins, and he was raised to life to make us right with God.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So, since we have been made right with God by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Through Jesus we have been brought, by faith, into this grace in which we now stand. And we are glad because of our hope of sharing in God's glory.
Not only that, but we are also glad in our troubles, because we know that trouble produces patience;
patience produces strong character; and strong character produces hope.
And this hope does not disappoint us, because God has poured out his love into our hearts through the Holy Spirit he has given us.
You see, at just the right time, when we were still powerless, Christ died for the ungodly.
It is rare for anyone to die even for a good person, though for a truly good person someone might dare to die.
But God shows his love for us in this way: while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
So now that we have been made right with God by Christ's blood, how much more will we be saved from God's anger through him!
For if, while we were God's enemies, we were brought back to him through the death of his Son, how much more, now that we are his friends, will we be saved through his life!
Not only that, but we are also glad in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now been brought back to God.
So then, sin came into the world through one man, and death came through sin. And so death spread to everyone, because everyone sinned.
Sin was in the world before the law was given. But sin is not counted against anyone when there is no law.
Yet death ruled from the time of Adam to the time of Moses, even over those who did not sin in the same way Adam did, when he broke God's command. Adam was a picture of the One who was coming.
But God's free gift is not like Adam's sin. Many died because of the sin of one man. But God's grace, and the gift that came by the grace of one man, Jesus Christ, overflowed to many even more.
And the gift is not like the result of one man's sin. The judgment after one sin brought condemnation, but the free gift brought a right standing, even after many sins.
For if death ruled because of one man's sin, how much more will those who receive God's overflowing grace and gift of right standing rule in life through one man, Jesus Christ!
So then, just as one sin brought condemnation for everyone, one act of right brought a right standing and life for everyone.
For just as many were made sinners through the disobedience of one man, so many will be made right with God through the obedience of one man.
The law was brought in so that sin would increase. But where sin increased, grace increased even more,
so that, just as sin ruled and brought death, grace might rule by making people right with God, bringing eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So what should we say? Should we keep on sinning so that grace can grow?
No, never! We died to sin. So how can we go on living in it?
Don't you know that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
So through baptism we were buried with him into death, so that, just as Christ was raised from death by the glory of the Father, we too may live a new life.
For if we have become joined with him in a death like his, we will also be joined with him in a resurrection like his.
We know that our old self was nailed to the cross with him, so that the body ruled by sin would lose its power, and we would no longer be slaves to sin.
For anyone who has died has been set free from sin.
Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him.
We know that Christ, who was raised from death, will never die again; death no longer has power over him.
The death he died, he died to sin once for all. But the life he lives, he lives to God.
In the same way, count yourselves dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
So do not let sin rule in your body, which will die, by obeying its desires.

Do not give any part of yourself to sin to be used for evil. Instead, give yourselves to God, as people who have been brought from death to life. Give every part of yourself to God to be used for what is right.
For sin will not rule over you, because you are not under the law, but under grace.
So what then? Should we sin because we are not under the law, but under grace? No, never!

Don't you know that when you give yourself to someone to obey, you are the slave of the one you obey? You are either a slave to sin, which leads to death, or a slave to obedience, which leads to right living.
But thanks be to God! You used to be slaves to sin, but you obeyed with all your heart the teaching you were given.
Now you have been set free from sin and have become slaves to right living.

I am using an everyday example, because you are weak in your human nature. Just as you once gave yourselves to be slaves to impurity and to more and more evil, now give yourselves to be slaves to right living, leading to holiness.
When you were slaves to sin, you were free from doing what is right.
What did you gain from the things you are now ashamed of? Those things lead to death!
But now you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God. The good you gain leads to holiness, and the end is eternal life.
For the payment for sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
Brothers and sisters, I know you all understand the Law of Moses. So surely you know that the law rules over people only while they are alive.
The laws say that a woman who is married is her husband’s wife for as long as he is alive. But if her husband dies, then she is free. That what the laws say.

The laws say she commits adultery if she marries another man while her husband lives. But if her husband dies, she is free from the laws. If she marries another man then, she does not commit adultery.

In the same way, my brothers and sisters, your old selves died and you became free from the law through the body of Christ. Now you belong to someone else. You belong to the one who was raised from death. We belong to Christ so that we can be used in service to God
In the past we were ruled by our sinful selves. The law made us want to do sinful things. And those sinful desires controlled our bodies, so that what we did only brought us death.
In the past the law held us as prisoners, but our old selves died, and we were made free from the law. So now we serve God in a new way, not in the old way, with the written rules.

Some people say: Is God’s laws are sin? No, it is wrong to say that. I know God’s laws and that is why I know what sin is. For example, God’s laws say: You must not want something that belongs to someone else.
But sin uses these laws and makes me feel like I want to have it. If there were no laws, then sin could not make me want to have those things that are wrong.
I was once alive without knowledge of the Law; but when I understood the meaning of the commandment, sin became alive and I died since the Law sentenced me to death
God gave the laws to make me alive, but what happened was that those laws brought me death.
Sin found a way to fool me by using the command to make me die.
Now the law is holy, and the command is holy and right and good.

Does this mean that something that is good brought death to me? No, it was sin that used the good command to bring me death. This shows how terrible sin really is. It can use a good command to produce a result that shows sin at its very worst.
We know that the law is spiritual, but I am not. I am so human. Sin rules me as if I were its slave.
I do not understand what I am doing. I don’t do the good I want to do, and I do the evil I hate.
And if I don’t want to do what I do, that means I agree that the law is good.
But I am not really the one doing the evil. It is sin living in me that does it.
I know that there is nothing in me that is good. I am sinful. I want to do good things, but I don’t do them.
I don’t do the good that I want to do. I do the evil that I don’t want to do.
So if I do what I don’t want to do, then I am not really the one doing it. It is the sin living in me that does it.
So I have learned this rule: When I want to do good, evil is there with me
In my mind I am happy with God’s law
But I see another law working in my body. That law makes war against the law that my mind accepts. That other law working in my body is the law of sin, and that law makes me its prisoner.
What a miserable person I am! Who will save me from this body that brings me death?
I thank God for his salvation through Jesus Christ our Lord! So, in my mind I servant to God’s law, but in my sinful self I am a slave to the law of sin.
So now anyone who is in Christ Jesus is not judged guilty. Who not follow our sinful ways but follow the ways of the spirit.
It is because in Christ Jesus the law of the Spirit brings life and has made you free. The Spirit made you free from the law that brings sin and death

God did what his laws could not do. He saved us. The law was without power because it was made weak by our sinful flesh. But God sent his Son, and He became a Man, a human like the people who sin, and God punished Him for their sin. That is how God punished the sin which ruled over people.
God did this so that we could be right just as the law said we must be. Now we do not follow our sinful selves. We follow the Spirit.
People who follow their sinful selves think only about what they want. But those who follow the Spirit are thinking about what the Spirit wants them to do.
If you’re thinking is controlled by your sinful self, there is death. But if you’re thinking is controlled by the Spirit, there is life and peace.
Anyone whose thinking is controlled by their sinful self is against God. They refuse to obey God’s law. They are not able to obey it.
Those who are ruled by their sinful selves cannot please God.
But you are not ruled by your sinful selves. You are ruled by the Spirit, that Spirit of God really lives in you. But whoever does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to Christ.
Your bodies will die because sin. But if Christ lives in you, then the Spirit gives you life, because Christ made you right with God.

Jesus died, but the Spirit of God made Him live again. That same Spirit lives in your hearts and that is why He will also make you live again, you who have bodies that will die. The Spirit of God who lives in your heart will raise you as He raised Jesus.
So, my brothers and sisters, we have a responsibility not to be ruled by our sinful selves. We must not live the way our sinful selves want.
If you use live to do what your sinful selves want, you will die. But if you use the Spirit’s help to stop doing the wrong things you do with your body, you will have true life.
The true children of God are those who let God’s Spirit lead them.
The Spirit that we received is not a spirit that makes us slaves again to fear. The Spirit that we have makes us adopted children of God. And with that Spirit we cry out, Abba, Father.
And the Spirit himself speaks to our spirits and makes us sure that we are God’s children.

If we are God’s children, we will get the blessings God has for his people. He will give us all that he has given Christ. But we must suffer like Christ suffered. Then we will also be glorified with him.
We have to suffer now, But God will give us something much better, something wonderful, and we will see it.
Everything that God has made is waiting with excitement for the time when he will show the world who his children are.
Everything God made was allowed to become like something that cannot fulfill its purpose. That was not its choice, but God made it happen with this hope in view:
That the creation would be made free from ruin. That everything God made would have the same freedom and glory that belong to God’s children.
We know that everything God made has been waiting until now in pain like a woman ready to give birth to a child.

Not only the world, but we also have been waiting with pain inside us. We have the Spirit as the first part of God’s promise. So, we are waiting for God to finish making us his own children. I mean we are waiting for our bodies to be made free.
God has saved us, and He gave us this hope, to wait for something wonderful. If we can see what we are waiting for, that is not really hope. People don’t hope for something they already have.
But we are hoping for something we don’t have yet, and we are waiting for it patiently.

Also, the Spirit helps us. We are weak, but the Spirit helps us with our weakness. We don’t know how to pray as we should, but the Spirit himself speaks to God for us. He begs God for us, speaking to him with feelings too deep for words.
God already knows our deepest thoughts. And he understands what the Spirit is saying, because the Spirit speaks for his people in the way that agrees with what God wants.
The people who love God should know that everything that happens to them is for good. God makes it happen that way because that was his plan.
God knew them before he made the world. And he decided that they would be like his Son. Then Jesus would be the firstborn of many brothers and sisters.
God planned for them to be like his Son. God calls them to himself, and God made them right with him. And after he made them right, he gave them his glory.
So what should we say about this? If God is for us, no one can stand against us.
He even let his own Son suffer for us. God gave his Son for all of us. So now with Jesus, God will freely give us all things.
Who can accuse the people God? No one! God is the one who makes them right.
Who can say that God’s people are guilty? No one! Christ Jesus died for us, but that is not all. Jesus was also raised from death. And now he is at God’s right side, speaking to God for us.
Can anything separate us from Christ’s love? Can trouble or problems or persecution. Can no food or no clothes or face danger or even death?
As the scripture says, For your sake we are in danger of death at all times; we are treated like sheep that are going to be slaughtered.

No, in all these things we have complete victory through Jesus who loved us. Romans 8 38 For I am certain that nothing can separate us from his love: neither death nor life, neither angels nor other heavenly rulers or powers, neither the present nor the future,
Neither the world above nor the world below there is nothing in all creation that will ever be able to separate us from the love of God which is ours through Christ Jesus our Lord.

9.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I am telling the truth in Christ; I am not lying. My conscience, guided by the Holy Spirit, agrees with me:
I have great sorrow and constant pain in my heart.
For I could almost wish that I myself were cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my own people, my own flesh and blood.
They are the people of Israel. God made them his children. He gave them his glory, his agreements, the law, the worship, and the promises.
They are descended from the great ancestors, and from them, as a human being, came Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever. Amen.
It is not as though God's word has failed. For not everyone descended from Israel really belongs to Israel.
And not all of Abraham's descendants are his true children. Instead, God said: Your descendants will come through Isaac.
In other words, it is not the natural children who are God's children. It is the children of the promise who are counted as Abraham's descendants.
For this is what the promise said: At the right time I will come back, and Sarah will have a son.
Not only that, but Rebecca's two children had the same father, our ancestor Isaac.
Yet before the children were born, before they had done anything good or bad — so that God's plan to choose people might stand, not based on works but on the One who calls —
she was told: The older will serve the younger.
As the Scripture says: I loved Jacob, but I rejected Esau.
So what should we say? Is God unfair? No, never!
For God said to Moses: I will have mercy on whomever I choose, and I will show pity to whomever I choose.
So it does not depend on human wanting or human effort, but on God, who shows mercy.
For the Scripture says to Pharaoh: I raised you up for this very reason: so that I might show my power through you, and so that my name might be told all over the earth.
So then, God has mercy on whomever he chooses, and he hardens whomever he chooses.
So you might say to me: Then why does God still blame us? For who can resist his will?
But who are you, a mere human, to argue with God? Should the thing that was made say to the one who made it: Why did you make me like this?
Does the potter not have the right to make, from the same lump of clay, one pot for special use and another for ordinary use?
What if God, wanting to show his anger and make his power known, patiently put up with the objects of his anger, which were ready to be destroyed?
What if he did this to make known the riches of his glory to the objects of his mercy, whom he prepared in advance for glory —
that is, to us, whom he called, not only from the Jews but also from the other nations?
As God says in Hosea: I will call them my people who were not my people, and I will call her my loved one who was not loved.
And: In the very place where they were told, You are not my people, they will be called children of the living God.
And Isaiah cries out about Israel: Even if the people of Israel are as many as the sand of the sea, only a few will be saved.
For the Lord will carry out his sentence on the earth quickly and completely.
As Isaiah also said before: If the Lord of heaven's armies had not left us some descendants, we would have become like Sodom, and would have been like Gomorrah.
So what should we say? That the other nations, who were not even chasing after a right standing with God, have received it — a right standing that comes by faith.
But Israel, who chased after a law that would make them right, did not reach it.
Why not? Because they did not seek it by faith, but as if it could come by works. They tripped over the stumbling stone,
as the Scripture says: Look, I am laying in Zion a stone that makes people trip and a rock that makes them fall. But the one who trusts in him will never be put to shame.

10.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Brothers and sisters, the deep desire of my heart, and my prayer to God, is for the people of Israel to be saved.
I can say about them that they are very eager for God, but their eagerness is not based on real knowledge.
They did not understand how God makes people right with himself, and they tried to set up their own way of being right. So they did not accept God's way.
For Christ is the end of the law, so that everyone who believes may be made right with God.
Moses writes about being made right by the law: The person who obeys these things will live by them.
But being made right by faith says this: Do not say in your heart, Who will go up to heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down)
or, Who will go down into the deep? (that is, to bring Christ up from death).
Instead, what does it say? The message is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart. This is the message of faith that we preach:
If you say with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised him from death, you will be saved.
For it is by believing in your heart that you are made right with God, and it is by saying it with your mouth that you are saved.
As the Scripture says: The one who trusts in him will never be put to shame.
There is no difference between Jew and Greek. The same Lord is Lord of all, and he richly blesses all who call on him,
for: Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.
But how can people call on the One they have not believed in? And how can they believe in the One they have not heard about? And how can they hear without someone preaching?
And how can anyone preach unless he is sent? As the Scripture says: How beautiful are the feet of those who bring the good news!
But not everyone has accepted the good news. For Isaiah says: Lord, who has believed our message?
So faith comes from hearing the message, and the message is heard through the word of Christ.
But I ask: Did they not hear? Yes, they did: Their voice went out into all the earth, their words to the ends of the world.
Again I ask: Did Israel not understand? First, Moses says: I will make you jealous of those who are not even a nation; I will make you angry with a nation that has no understanding.
And Isaiah boldly says: I was found by those who were not looking for me; I showed myself to those who were not asking for me.
But about Israel he says: All day long I have held out my hands to a people who disobey and argue.

11.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So I ask: Did God reject his people? No, never! I myself am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
God did not reject his people, whom he chose long ago. Don't you know what the Scripture says about Elijah, how he pleaded with God against Israel:
Lord, they have killed your prophets and torn down your altars. I am the only one left, and they are trying to kill me?
And what was God's answer to him? I have kept for myself seven thousand who have not bowed down to worship Baal.
So in the same way, at the present time, there is a small group that God has chosen by his grace.
And if it is by grace, then it is no longer based on works; if it were, grace would not be grace.
So what then? The people of Israel did not get what they were looking for. The chosen ones got it, but the rest were made hard,
as the Scripture says: God gave them a dull spirit, eyes that do not see and ears that do not hear, right up to this very day.
And David says: Let their feasts become a trap and a snare, a stumbling block and a punishment for them.
Let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and let their backs be bent forever.
So I ask: Did the people stumble so badly that they fell beyond recovery? No, never! Instead, because they slipped, salvation has come to the other nations, to make Israel jealous.
Now if their slipping means riches for the world, and their loss means riches for the other nations, how much greater riches will their full return bring!
I am speaking to you who are not Jews. As the apostle to the other nations, I take pride in my work,
hoping somehow to make my own people jealous and so save some of them.
For if their being set aside means the world being brought back to God, what will their being welcomed back mean, but life from the dead?
If the first piece of dough offered to God is holy, then the whole batch is holy. And if the root is holy, so are the branches.
Some of the branches have been broken off, and you, a wild olive branch, have been grafted in among the others. Now you share in the rich life of the olive root.
So do not brag against those branches. If you do, remember this: you do not hold up the root; the root holds you up.
You might say: Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.
That is true. They were broken off because of unbelief, and you stand only by faith. So do not be proud, but be careful.
For if God did not spare the natural branches, he will not spare you either.
So look at both the kindness and the strictness of God: he was strict with those who fell, but kind to you — if you continue in his kindness. Otherwise, you also will be cut off.
And if they do not continue in unbelief, they will be grafted in again, because God is able to graft them in again.
After all, you were cut from a wild olive tree and grafted, against nature, into a cultivated one. How much more easily will these natural branches be grafted back into their own olive tree!
Brothers and sisters, I want you to understand this secret, so that you will not think you are wise: Israel has become hard in part, until the full number of the other nations has come in.
And in this way all Israel will be saved, as the Scripture says: The Deliverer will come from Zion; he will turn the people of Jacob away from sin.
And this is my agreement with them: I will take away their sins.
As far as the good news is concerned, they are God's enemies for your sake. But as far as God's choosing is concerned, they are loved because of the ancestors,
for God never takes back his gifts or his calling.
At one time you did not obey God, but now you have received mercy because they did not obey.
In the same way, they have now disobeyed, so that they too may now receive mercy, because of the mercy shown to you.
For God has allowed everyone to be bound up in disobedience, so that he can have mercy on everyone.
Oh, how deep are the riches and the wisdom and the knowledge of God! No one can fully understand his decisions or trace out his ways!
For: Who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been his adviser?
Who has ever given to God, so that God would owe him anything?
For everything comes from him, exists through him, and is for him. To him be glory forever. Amen.

Brothers and sisters in Christ, I beg you, because of the great mercy God has shown us, offer your lives as a living sacrifice to God, an offering that is only for God and pleasing to him. Considering what he has done, it is only right that you should worship him in this way.
Do not be like the people of this world, but let God change you with a new way of thinking. Then you will know what the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God is.

God was very good to me and that is why I am saying to each of you: Do not think you are more important than you really are. You must be wise when you think of yourselves and remember it is God who tells each believer how he must serve Him.
All of us together are like a human body. A man has one body but his body has many parts and each part is doing something else.
It is the same with Us. There are many believers, but we all belong to Christ and that is why we are like a body. We all belong to each other.

God gave each one of us gifts, but God did not give the same gifts to each one of us. If God gave some believers the gift of preaching the messages of God, then they must tell people the right message that we believe.
If God gave some other believers the gift of serving, then they must help and serve other people. If God gave some believers the gift of teaching other people, then they must teach them about God.

If God gave some believers the gift of comforting other people, Whoever has the gift of comforting others should do that. Whoever has the gift of giving to help others should give generously. Whoever has the gift of leading should work hard at it. Whoever has the gift of showing kindness to others should do it gladly.
Your love must be real. You must hate evil and hold tight to good things.
Love each other in a way that makes you feel close like brothers and sisters. And give each other more honor than you give yourself.
As you serve the Lord, work hard and don’t be lazy. Be excited about serving him!
Be happy because of the hope you have. Be patient when you have troubles. Pray all the time.
Some of the believers are poor. Help them and take care of them. You must be friendly to strangers.
When people make you suffer and make trouble for you, ask the Lord to bless them. Don’t ask Him to punish them.
When other people are happy, be happy with them and rejoice. When they are sad, be sad and cry with them.

Live together in peace with one another and work together. Don’t be proud but be willing to be friends with people who are not important to others. Don’t think of yourself as smarter than everyone else.
When people do bad things to you, do not try to do bad things to them. You must always do what is right. Try to do what everyone thinks is right.
Try very hard to live in peace with other people.

Dear friends, when people make you suffer and do bad things to you, do not try to punish them yourselves. Wait for God to punish them, because it is written in the Old Testament: The Lord said: I am the One who will punish people for what they have done wrong.
But you should do this: When your enemies are hungry, give them food to eat, and when they are thirsty, give them something to drink. If you do that. In doing this you will make them feel ashamed.
Don’t let evil defeat you, but defeat evil by doing good.

13.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Everyone should obey the governing authorities, because all authority comes from God. The authorities that exist were put there by God.
So anyone who fights against the authority is fighting against what God has set up, and those who do this will bring judgment on themselves.
For rulers do not frighten those who do right, but those who do wrong. Do you want to be free from fear of the one in authority? Then do what is right, and he will praise you.
For he is God's servant, working for your good. But if you do wrong, be afraid, because he does not carry the sword for nothing. He is God's servant, who carries out God's anger on the wrongdoer.
So you must obey, not only to avoid being punished, but also because it is the right thing to do.
This is also why you pay taxes, for the authorities are God's servants, who give their full time to governing.
Give everyone what you owe them: pay your taxes and fees, and give respect and honor to those they are due.
Do not owe anyone anything, except to love one another. For whoever loves others has fully obeyed the law.
The commands — Do not commit adultery, Do not murder, Do not steal, Do not want what belongs to others — and any other command, are all summed up in this one rule: Love your neighbor as yourself.
Love does no harm to a neighbor. So love is how the law is fully obeyed.
And do this because you know the time we live in. The hour has come for you to wake up from sleep, because our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.
The night is almost over; the day is near. So let us get rid of the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.
Let us live properly, as in the daytime — not in wild parties and drunkenness, not in sexual sin and wild living, not in fighting and jealousy.
Instead, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and do not think about how to satisfy the wrong desires of your sinful self.

14.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Welcome anyone whose faith is weak, but do not argue with him about matters of opinion.
One person believes he may eat anything, while another, whose faith is weak, eats only vegetables.
The one who eats everything must not look down on the one who does not, and the one who does not eat everything must not judge the one who does, because God has welcomed him.
Who are you to judge someone else's servant? Whether he stands or falls is his own master's concern. And he will stand, because the Lord is able to make him stand.
One person thinks one day is more holy than another, while another thinks every day is the same. Each person should be fully convinced in his own mind.

Whoever treats a certain day as special does it for the Lord. Whoever eats anything does it for the Lord, because he gives thanks to God. And whoever does not eat certain things does that for the Lord too, and gives thanks to God.
For none of us lives only for himself, and none of us dies only for himself.
If we live, we live for the Lord, and if we die, we die for the Lord. So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord.
For this is why Christ died and came back to life: so that he would be Lord of both the dead and the living.
So why do you judge your brother or sister? And why do you look down on them? We will all stand before God to be judged.
For the Scripture says: As surely as I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow before me, and every tongue will give praise to God.
So then, each of us will give an account of himself to God.
So let us stop judging one another. Instead, decide this: do not put anything in your brother's way that would make him trip or fall.
I know, and the Lord Jesus has convinced me, that no food is unclean in itself. But if someone believes a thing is unclean, then for him it is unclean.
If your brother is hurt by what you eat, you are no longer acting in love. Do not destroy, by what you eat, someone for whom Christ died.
Do not let what you know is good be spoken of as evil.
For the kingdom of God is not about food and drink, but about right living, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
Anyone who serves Christ in this way pleases God and is approved by people.
So let us go after the things that lead to peace and that build one another up.
Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All food is clean, but it is wrong for a person to eat anything that causes someone else to fall.
It is better not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that makes your brother or sister fall.
Whatever you believe about these things, keep between yourself and God. Happy is the person who does not feel guilty about what he allows himself to do.
But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats, because he is not acting from faith. And anything that is not from faith is sin.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: We who are strong should help carry the weaknesses of those who are weak, and not just please ourselves.
Each of us should please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.
For even Christ did not please himself. As the Scripture says: The insults of those who insulted you fell on me.
Everything that was written in the past was written to teach us, so that through the patience and comfort the Scriptures give, we might have hope.
May God, who gives patience and comfort, help you to live in harmony with one another, following Christ Jesus,
so that with one heart and one voice you may give glory to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
So welcome one another, just as Christ welcomed you, to the glory of God.
For I tell you that Christ became a servant of the Jews, to show that God is true, and to confirm the promises made to the ancestors,
and also so that the other nations might give glory to God for his mercy. As the Scripture says: So I will praise you among the nations; I will sing praises to your name.
And again it says: Be glad, you nations, together with his people.
And again: Praise the Lord, all you nations; let all the peoples praise him.
And Isaiah says: There will come a root from Jesse, one who will rise to rule the nations; the nations will put their hope in him.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him, so that you will overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
My brothers and sisters, I myself am sure that you are full of goodness and knowledge, and that you are able to teach one another.
Still, I have written to you quite boldly on some points, to remind you of them again, because of the grace God gave me

to be a servant of Christ Jesus to the other nations. I have the priestly duty of preaching the good news of God, so that the nations might become an offering that pleases God, made holy by the Holy Spirit.
So I am proud of what I have done for God, in Christ Jesus.
I will not dare to talk about anything except what Christ has done through me to bring the nations to obey God — by what I have said and done,
by the power of signs and wonders, through the power of the Spirit of God. So from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum, I have fully preached the good news of Christ.
It has always been my goal to preach the good news where Christ was not already known, so that I would not be building on someone else's foundation.
Instead, as the Scripture says: Those who were not told about him will see, and those who have not heard will understand.
This is also why I have so often been kept from coming to you.
But now there is no more place for me to work in these regions, and I have wanted for many years to come to you.
So I plan to do this when I go to Spain. I hope to see you as I pass through, and to have you help me on my way there, after I have enjoyed being with you for a while.
But right now I am on my way to Jerusalem to help God's people there.
For the believers in Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a gift for the poor among God's people in Jerusalem.
They were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the other nations have shared in the spiritual blessings of the Jews, they ought to share their material blessings with them.
So after I have finished this task and made sure they receive this gift, I will go to Spain and visit you on the way.
I know that when I come to you, I will come with the full blessing of Christ.
Brothers and sisters, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that the Spirit gives, I beg you to join me in my struggle by praying to God for me.
Pray that I will be kept safe from those in Judea who do not believe, and that the help I bring to Jerusalem will be welcomed by God's people there.
Then, by God's will, I can come to you with joy and be refreshed together with you.
May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I am introducing to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church in Cenchrea.
Welcome her in the Lord, as God's people should, and help her with anything she needs from you, because she has been a great help to many people, including me.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus.
They risked their own lives for me. Not only I, but all the churches of the other nations are thankful for them.
Greet also the church that meets in their house. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Achaia to believe in Christ.
Greet Mary, who worked very hard for you.
Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives who were in prison with me. They are respected among the apostles, and they belonged to Christ before I did.
Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
Greet Apelles, who has proven himself faithful in Christ. Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.
Greet Herodion, my relative. Greet those in the household of Narcissus who belong to the Lord.
Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis, who has also worked very hard for the Lord.
Greet Rufus, chosen by the Lord, and his mother, who has been a mother to me too.
Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters with them.
Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all God's people who are with them.
Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ send you their greetings.
Brothers and sisters, I beg you to watch out for those who cause divisions and put obstacles in your way, against the teaching you have learned. Stay away from them.
For such people are not serving our Lord Christ, but their own appetites. By smooth talk and flattery they fool the minds of innocent people.
Everyone has heard about your obedience, so I am very glad about you. But I want you to be wise about what is good, and innocent about what is evil.
The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. May the grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
Timothy, my fellow worker, sends his greetings, and so do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
I, Tertius, who wrote down this letter, also greet you in the Lord.
Gaius, whose home I am staying in, and who hosts the whole church, sends you greetings. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus send you greetings.
May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
Now to God, who is able to make you strong by the good news I preach and by the message about Jesus Christ — the secret that was kept hidden for long ages,
but is now revealed and made known through the prophets' writings, by the command of the eternal God, so that all nations might believe and obey him —
to the only wise God be glory forever through Jesus Christ! Amen.
